---
title: "Variable_Creation"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-16"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## Variable Creation

The dataset used in this analysis, `Violent_Crimes.csv`, contains incidents classified as violent crimes based on LAPD crime codes. For this study, only variables relevant to the research questions were selected and, where necessary, transformed.

To analyze trends over time, the variable `DATE.OCC` (date of occurrence) was used to create two derived variables: **year** and **month**. The year variable allows aggregation of violent crime counts across years for trend analysis, while the month variable supports more detailed examination of patterns within each year.

To examine the geographic distribution of violent crime, the **reporting district number (`Rpt.Dist.No`)** was used as the primary variable for district-level analysis. Additionally, **area identifiers (`AREA` and `AREA.NAME`)** were used to provide more interpretable geographic labels in tables and visualizations.

Descriptive variables were also included to summarize characteristics of the incidents, including **victim age (`Vict.Age`)**, **victim sex (`Vict.Sex`)**, and **victim descent (`Vict.Descent`)**. The **crime code (`Crm.Cd`)** and **crime description (`Crm.Cd.Desc`)** were used to identify the types of violent crimes.

Finally, aggregated variables such as **total violent crimes per year** and **total violent crimes per reporting district** were computed to support statistical summaries and visualizations used in the analysis.

```{r variable-creation}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

violent_crime_data <- read.csv("Violent_Crimes.csv")

violent_crime_data <- violent_crime_data %>%
  mutate(
    DATE.OCC = as.Date(DATE.OCC),
    year = year(DATE.OCC),
    month = month(DATE.OCC, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE)
  )
```

```{r summaries}
# Yearly totals
yearly_crime <- violent_crime_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(total_violent_crimes = n())

yearly_crime

# District totals
district_crime <- violent_crime_data %>%
  group_by(Rpt.Dist.No) %>%
  summarise(total_violent_crimes = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_violent_crimes))

head(district_crime, 10)
```


```{r}
```
